title: antirrhinum hispanicum subsp. australe
intro: Antirrhinum hispanicum subsp. australe, also known as the Australian snapdragon or pink trumpet flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial in treating various health conditions, including respiratory issues, wounds, and fever. The health benefits of Antirrhinum hispanicum subsp. australe include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe coughs, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
